mnemonics.title: Critical Section Requirements
mnemonics.content: MUTEX-P: Mutual Exclusion, Uniform Progress, Termination, EXit, Bounded waiting, Progress (M-UTEX-P)

mnemonics.title: Race Condition Prevention
mnemonics.content: SYNCHRONIZE: SYNchronize access, Use atomic ops, NOtice timing, CHeck shared data, ROhibit concurrent access, OOnly one in CS, NCoherent reads, IImplement locks, ZZero race conditions

acronyms.usage: The portion of code accessing shared resources that must be executed by only one process at a time
Requirement that no process should wait to enter CS if none are currently in it
Requirement that limits how many times other processes can enter CS after a process requests entry
Atomic hardware instruction used for implementing mutual exclusion locks
Classical problem where readers can access concurrently but writers need exclusive access

visualTips.visualization: Imagine two people simultaneously trying to update the same bank account balance. If both read $1000, both add $100, and both write back, final balance shows $1100 instead of $1200 - the $100 update is 'lost'
Picture a single restroom with a lock. Only one person can enter at a time. The lock ensures they have exclusive access to the shared 'resource' (the restroom)
Think of a semaphore as a ticket counter. wait() takes a ticket (decrements), signal() returns a ticket (increments). If no tickets left, you must wait in line.
Deadlock = Two cars at intersection, each waiting for other to move (forever blocked). Race Condition = Two cars arriving at different times, whoever arrives first gets through, outcome unpredictable

analogies.analogy: Two cooks adding ingredients to the same pot without knowing what the other added - final dish may have too much or too little of something depending on timing
Only one person can use the combination lock safe at a time - otherwise they'll overwrite each other's access codes
Mutex is like a personal bathroom key - only the person who took it can return it. Binary semaphore is like a public restroom sign - anyone can flip it from occupied to vacant
In a queue for movie tickets with 'fair' system: after you request a ticket, only a limited number of people ahead of you can get tickets before you're served
